# Algorand Explorers and Dashboards

## Allo

Allo Explorer is a modern, full-featured blockchain explorer for Algorand, created by AlgoNode and designed to make on-chain data actually understandable. It launched in late 2023 with a mission to make Algorand's data accessible to everyone, not just protocol nerds.

Allo lets you search and inspect:
- Assets (fungible tokens, NFTs, RWAs)
- Accounts
- Apps / smart contracts
- Blocks & transactions
- NFDomains
- Ecosystem projects (lightweight ecosystem directory)
- Inspect and analyze TEAL smart contracts directly in the UI.

It also provides real-time network metrics like circulating supply, block time, and TPS.

- Allo Website: https://allo.info/
- Allo Resources: https://allo.info/resources

## Algo Surf

Algo Surf is a suite of Algorand blockchain analysis tools, not just a basic explorer. It's designed for users who want deeper visibility into ALGO flows, wallet behavior, and transaction patterns.

What Algo Surf includes:

**Flow** (https://flow.algo.surf/)
A blockchain analysis tool that lets you track ALGO inflows and outflows for any address. You can see:
- Incoming/outgoing ALGO
- Transaction history
- Counterparties
- Net flow over time
- Patterns of activity

**Clerk** (https://clerk.algo.surf/)
A utility for exporting ALGO and ASA transactions to CSV – useful for accounting, taxes, audits, and portfolio tracking.

**Batch** (https://batch.algo.surf/)
A tool for multi-ALGO balance lookups, letting you check balances across many addresses at once.

What Algo Surf Is Not:
It's not a full general-purpose explorer like Allo or Pera Explorer. Instead, Algo Surf is more of a specialized analytics suite focused on money flow, wallet behavior, bulk data operations, exporting and reporting.

- Algo Surf Website: https://algo.surf/

## Lora

Lora the Explorer is a visual, interactive explorer that lets you inspect any Algorand network — LocalNet, TestNet, or MainNet — with a level of detail tailored specifically for developers. It doubles as both a network explorer (blocks, transactions, assets, accounts, apps), and a smart contract development tool (deploy, simulate, call, and debug apps).

- Lora Website: https://lora.algokit.io/
- GitHub (AlgoKit-Lora): https://github.com/algorandfoundation/algokit-lora

## Pera Explorer

Pera Explorer is the Algorand blockchain explorer built and maintained by the Pera Wallet team. It launched to replace AlgoExplorer after its shutdown, providing the Algorand community with a modern, reliable, and user-centric block explorer.

Pera Explorer lets you browse and inspect everything happening on the Algorand blockchain, including:
- Transactions (with full detail pages)
- Inner transactions
- Assets (ASAs)
- Addresses & balances
- Applications (smart contracts)
- Blocks & rounds

Links:
- Pera Explorer Website (Mainnet): https://explorer.perawallet.app/
- Pera Explorer Website (Testnet): https://testnet.explorer.perawallet.app/

## Nodely Dashboard

Nodely Dashboard for Algorand is essentially a real-time performance and infrastructure analytics hub built by Nodely, the team running globally distributed Algorand nodes and indexers. It gives developers, ecosystem operators, and protocol watchers a clean, high-resolution view into how the network is behaving under the hood. It's a collection of network-level metrics and explorer-style insights, including:

- TPS analysis — real-time throughput, peak vs. sustained TPS, and comparative views.
- Node and decentralization metrics — node counts, distribution, ledger size, and consensus participation.
- Ledger and archival data views — deep dives into ledger size, archival growth, and block characteristics.
- Network health indicators — latency, finality, and reliability signals

Links:
- Nodely Dashboard Website: https://g.nodely.io/dashboards
- Nodely Public API: https://nodely.io/docs/free/start

## DeFi Llama

DeFi Llama is a cross-chain analytics platform that tracks decentralized finance activity across hundreds of blockchains. For Algorand specifically, it provides a data-driven snapshot of the chain's economic activity, including TVL, stablecoin supply, fees, revenue, DEX volume, and protocol-level metrics.

- DeFi Llama Algorand Website: https://defillama.com/chain/algorand

## Algorand Metrics Portal

Algorand Metrics Portal is a curated, real-time hub that aggregates the most important on-chain, ecosystem, and sustainability data about the Algorand network. It pulls together analytics from Nodely, Chainspect, DeFi Llama, Nansen, Vestige, StakingRewards, Allo, and more, giving a single jumping-off point for anyone tracking network health, economic activity, or other metrics.

- Website: https://algorand.co/metrics

## Algorand Energy Dashboard

Algorand Energy Dashboard is an independent, open-source energy and emissions dashboard for the Algorand network, built by community member cusma. It visualizes how much power Algorand's node network consumes, how that translates into annualized energy use, and the resulting greenhouse-gas footprint — all using live or regularly updated on-chain node data.

- Algorand Energy Dashboard Website: https://cusma.github.io/algorand-energy/
